findings.title: Prototype pollution: Direct __proto__ access
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 9 co-located issues] Object merge/extend operation with user-controlled input can pollute Object.prototype.

**Analysis:**
🔍 Pattern: Direct __proto__ access
⚠️ User input from: `input`
📦 In function: `f` (7 callers)
findings.why_it_matters:
Prototype pollution allows attackers to inject properties into Object.prototype, which affects ALL objects in the application. This can lead to:
• Remote Code Execution (via gadget chains)
• Denial of Service
• Authentication bypass
• Property injection

findings.title: Potential ReDoS vulnerability
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 9 co-located issues] Regex with nested quantifiers may cause catastrophic backtracking, leading to denial of service.

**Analysis:**
🔍 Pattern issue: nested quantifiers detected
📝 Pattern: ``
📦 In function: `f` (7 callers)
🎯 User input detected in context
findings.why_it_matters:
ReDoS attacks exploit regex backtracking to consume CPU:
• A malicious input can take exponential time to evaluate
• Single requests can freeze the server
• Input like 'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!' on /(a+)+$/ can hang

findings.title: Test code in production: 1 issue
findings.description:
Test utilities and patterns found in what appears to be production code.

**Found:**
• Test code (line 35)
⚠️ This file is imported by production code!
findings.suggested_fix:
Options:

1. **Move to test files:** If this is test code, move it to a test directory
2. **Use TYPE_CHECKING:** For type hints only, wrap imports in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`
3. **Environment check:** If needed at runtime, guard with environment variable

```python
# Instead of:
from unittest.mock import Mock

# Use:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
if TYPE_CHECKING:
from unittest.mock import Mock
```
findings.why_it_matters: This test code is imported by production code. Test dependencies add bloat, security risks, and can cause unexpected behavior in production.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 3 functions in call cycle (complexity 40)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 3 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/retrofit/retrofit/src/main/java/retrofit2/RequestFactory.java::RequestFactory.Builder.build:186 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/retrofit/retrofit/src/main/java/retrofit2/RequestFactory.java::RequestFactory.Builder.parseHeaders:304 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/retrofit/retrofit/src/main/java/retrofit2/RequestFactory.java::RequestFactory.Builder.parseMethodAnnotation:232. Aggregate complexity: 40.
findings.suggested_fix: Break the cycle by extracting shared logic into a common helper, introducing an event system, or restructuring the call chain to be unidirectional.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: XSS: innerHTML assignment
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 3 co-located issues] **Direct innerHTML assignment can lead to XSS vulnerabilities**

**Location**: website/public/1.x/converter-jackson/script.js:23

**Code snippet**:
```
spanNode.innerHTML = tabs[value][1];
```

Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities occur when untrusted data is included
in web pages without proper validation or escaping. Attackers can inject malicious
scripts that:
- Steal user session cookies
- Capture keystrokes and credentials
- Redirect users to malicious sites
- Deface the application

This vulnerability is classified as **CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of
Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')**.
findings.why_it_matters: XSS vulnerabilities allow attackers to execute scripts in users' browsers, potentially stealing sensitive data or hijacking user sessions.

[COMPOUND: 3 co-located issues] **Direct innerHTML assignment can lead to XSS vulnerabilities**

**Location**: website/public/2.x/adapter-rxjava2/script.js:23

**Code snippet**:
```
spanNode.innerHTML = tabs[value][1];
```

Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities occur when untrusted data is included
in web pages without proper validation or escaping. Attackers can inject malicious
scripts that:
- Steal user session cookies
- Capture keystrokes and credentials
- Redirect users to malicious sites
- Deface the application

This vulnerability is classified as **CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of
Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')**.
findings.why_it_matters: XSS vulnerabilities allow attackers to execute scripts in users' browsers, potentially stealing sensitive data or hijacking user sessions.

Attackers can inject malicious scripts that: - Steal user session cookies - Capture keystrokes and credentials - Redirect users to malicious sites - Deface the application This vulnerability is classified as **CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')**.
findings.why_it_matters: XSS vulnerabilities allow attackers to execute scripts in users' browsers, potentially stealing sensitive data or hijacking user sessions.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 4 functions in call cycle (complexity 6)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 4 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/retrofit/retrofit/java-test/src/test/java/retrofit2/RequestFactoryTest.java::RequestFactoryTest.put:961 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/retrofit/retrofit/src/main/java/retrofit2/Retrofit.java::Retrofit.Builder.addConverterFactory:618 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/retrofit/retrofit/test-helpers/src/main/java/retrofit2/TestingUtils.java::TestingUtils.buildRequest:42 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/retrofit/samples/src/main/java/com/example/retrofit/AnnotatedConverters.java::AnnotatedConverters.AnnotatedConverterFactory.Builder.add:51. Aggregate complexity: 6.
findings.suggested_fix: Break the cycle by extracting shared logic into a common helper, introducing an event system, or restructuring the call chain to be unidirectional.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Hidden regex in loop: u
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 9 co-located issues] Function 'u' contains a loop and calls 'P' which compiles a regex.

**Call chain:** P → u → E

This may cause regex compilation on every iteration.
findings.suggested_fix:
Options:
1. Cache the compiled regex (lazy_static, OnceLock)
2. Pass pre-compiled regex as parameter
3. Restructure to compile once before loop
findings.why_it_matters: Hidden regex compilation across function boundaries is harder to spot but equally impactful.

findings.title: High Coupling: f (architectural hub)
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 9 co-located issues] Function 'f' has 317 connections (0 callers, 317 callees). High coupling increases change risk.

**Analysis:**
- In-degree (callers): 0
- Out-degree (callees): 317
- Total coupling: 317
findings.suggested_fix:
This is a coupling hotspot. Consider:
- Introducing abstraction layers
- Applying facade pattern
- Splitting by responsibility
findings.why_it_matters: Highly coupled code is harder to change and test. Changes cascade unpredictably.
